phase_stack.purpose: convert board research into objects/relations/transitions/constraints instead of answer injection
derive internal role structure and relative hierarchy under the board label
bind lawful controls into time-ordered replay with T+1 and no-leverage constraints
locate the main failure mode: selection, timing, sizing, or over-control
upgrade expression through probability/expectancy, soft-gate sizing, and constrained add/reduce
discover candidate high-dimensional states that may improve add/reduce/sizing without legislating them
decide whether the board exits as mainline asset, candidate, sidecar, or diagnostic-only

stop_conditions.trigger: board reaches mainline_asset / candidate / sidecar / diagnostic status
two consecutive upgrade loops fail to improve replay or audit evidence materially
missing lawful feed, owner label, or no-leak compatible episode inputs
discoveries remain sample-specific and fail stability/action-relevance judgement

promotion_gates.rule: only point-in-time visible labels, priors, and market context may influence training or replay decisions
candidate controls must survive time-ordered replay before promotion
sizing promotion must come from a stable zone, not a single lucky parameter point
candidate states discovered by vectors must pass stability, action relevance, boundary clarity, and incremental value judgement

execution_rules: run the full phase stack for each board in queue order without waiting for a new user prompt between phases
do not reopen already-frozen mainline assets unless a hard audit failure appears
allow single-board iteration loops only inside the board until one stop condition is hit
escalate to manual intervention only when a hard data block or promotion ambiguity remains after audit
treat single-symbol case work as mechanism discovery or residual repair, not as the primary training unit
interpretation: V1.14F turns the board-research method into a one-shot orchestration protocol: once a board enters the queue, it should keep moving through the fixed phase stack until a terminal status or hard stop appears.
This protocol is intentionally not an infinite research machine. It is a bounded board worker with explicit stop conditions, promotion gates, and lawful replay requirements.
The goal is to stop asking the user to reissue the same orchestration command every phase while still preserving auditability and anti-overfitting discipline.
